page: Microsoft Azure E4s_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ESv5 family. It is built on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and runs on an x86_64 architecture. The server features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz, with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU configuration includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data reveals a performance profile that leans towards average single-core execution but shows limitations under multi-threaded workloads. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance of 1897.19 and Geekbench single-core score of 1664.0 both rank in the average tier. However, multi-core metrics, such as the stress-ng multi-core CPU performance of 3873.38 and Geekbench multi-core score of 3527.0, fall into the weak category. Memory bandwidth tests also indicate constraints, with large-block read-only and write-only speeds registering at 29.84 GB/sec and 25.22 GB/sec respectively, both classified as weak.

This server is designed for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high parallel compute scaling. The 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio provides a cost-efficient profile for hosting relational databases, caching layers, or enterprise applications requiring a larger memory footprint relative to processing power. However, the weak multi-threaded performance and low memory bandwidth suggest it is less suitable for heavy parallel processing, software compilation, or high-throughput database operations. Appropriate workloads include static web serving, text processing, and lightweight memory-bound applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E4s_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine within the ESv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs from 2 physical Intel Xeon 8370C cores clocked at 2.8 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU resources. Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1664.0, but show weak multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth, as evidenced by a multi-core Geekbench score of 3527.0 and large-block read speeds of 29.84 GB/sec. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, low-compute workloads. It is best suited for memory-bound applications, static web serving, and moderate database operations that do not demand high parallel processing power.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E4s_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8370C processor, suited for memory-bound workloads with moderate compute needs.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E4s_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized VM with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, built for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio for memory-intensive workloads with moderate processing demands.
bullet_points: Features 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM on an Intel Xeon 8370C processor
High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core for memory-bound applications
Delivers average single-core performance but exhibits weak multi-core scaling
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on external Premium Storage
Best suited for static web serving, text processing, and moderate database tasks
